title: Route and tune differential pairs
description: Maintain constant intra-pair spacing, match lengths within the pair, and tune skew at the source of mismatch.
body: Use when routing differential interfaces such as USB, Ethernet, or LVDS. Do not let the two traces diverge in spacing or length, because mismatch converts differential signal into common-mode noise. Actions: route the pair with constant gap, match intra-pair length at the point of mismatch, and verify the differential impedance. Validation signals include intra-pair skew within the budget and a differential impedance reading on target. Risk boundary: excess skew radiates as common mode and can fail emissions, so escalate any pair exceeding the skew budget for re-tuning.
